name: The Great Gatsby
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
description: A story of wealth, love, and the American Dream in the Roaring Twenties.

author: George Orwell
description: A chilling prophecy about the future of surveillance and totalitarianism.

name: To Kill a Mockingbird
author: Harper Lee
description: A powerful tale of racial injustice and the loss of innocence in the American South.

name: The Hobbit
author: J.R.R. Tolkien
description: A hobbit's unexpected journey through Middle-earth to reclaim a treasure guarded by a dragon.

name: One Hundred Years of Solitude
author: Gabriel García Márquez
description: The multi-generational story of the Buendía family in the fictional town of Macondo.

name: Pride and Prejudice
author: Jane Austen
description: A sparkling comedy of manners centered on Elizabeth Bennet and Mr. Darcy.

name: The Catcher in the Rye
author: J.D. Salinger
description: Holden Caulfield's angst-filled journey through New York City after being expelled from prep school.

name: Brave New World
author: Aldous Huxley
description: A vision of a futuristic society driven by genetically modified citizens and social hierarchy.

name: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
description: A shepherd boy's journey to find treasure in the Egyptian pyramids and discovering himself along the way.

name: Crime and Punishment
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
description: A psychological exploration of the mental anguish and moral dilemmas of a former student who commits a crime.

name: The Chronicles of Narnia: The Lion, the Witch and the Wardrobe
author: C.S. Lewis
description: Four children step through a wardrobe into a magical land called Narnia.

name: Fahrenheit 451
author: Ray Bradbury
description: A future society where books are banned and 'firemen' burn any that are found.

name: Moby-Dick
author: Herman Melville
description: The obsessive quest of Captain Ahab for revenge on Moby Dick, the giant white whale.

name: The Picture of Dorian Gray
author: Oscar Wilde
description: A young man sells his soul so that his portrait ages while he remains eternally young.

name: The Handmaid's Tale
author: Margaret Atwood
description: A woman's struggle for survival and identity in a near-future totalitarian state.

name: The Brothers Karamazov
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
description: A complex story centered on a murder case and deep philosophical debates about God, free will, and morality.

name: Jane Eyre
author: Charlotte Brontë
description: The life experiences of the titular character, including her growth and her love for Mr. Rochester.

name: Frankenstein
author: Mary Shelley
description: A scientist creates a sentient creature in an unorthodox scientific experiment.

name: The Little Prince
author: Antoine de Saint-Exupéry
description: A pilot stranded in the desert meets a young prince who fell to Earth from a tiny asteroid.

name: Life of Pi
author: Yann Martel
description: A young boy survives a shipwreck and shares a lifeboat with a Bengal tiger named Richard Parker.

name: Animal Farm
author: George Orwell
description: A satirical allegorical novella that reflects events leading up to the Russian Revolution of 1917.

name: Down and Out in Paris and London
author: George Orwell
description: A memoir in two parts on the theme of poverty in the two cities, reflecting Orwell's own experiences.
